slides.title: Your body's cleanup network has *no pump*.
slides.sub: The lymphatic system clears waste and moves immune cells, but only when you do. Here is how to keep it flowing.

slides.title: A drainage system, no engine
slides.body: Your heart pumps blood. But lymph, the fluid that clears cellular waste, has *no pump of its own*.

slides.title: Muscles do the pumping
slides.body: Lymph relies on the squeeze of your muscles to move. When you sit still for hours, the flow *slows to a crawl*.

slides.title: It carries your defenses
slides.body: This network ferries immune cells and drains waste. Keeping it moving *can support* how well your body cleans itself.

slides.title: Your second heart
slides.body: Calf muscles act like a pump for the lower body. Every step you take *helps push fluid* back up toward the core.

slides.title: Walk, often
slides.body: You do not need a workout. A short walk, a few stair climbs, standing up each hour: small movement *keeps it flowing*.

slides.title: Keep the *cleanup crew* moving.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps, movement and daily activity, then folds them into one Health Score so the small habits actually add up.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your lymphatic system is the body's quiet cleanup crew, and it has no pump of its own. It moves only when you do. 🚶🌿

Muscle movement, especially the calves, drains waste and ferries immune cells through this hidden network. Walk often, sit less, and it keeps flowing. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your steps and daily movement into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Cleanup System That Only Works When You Move
